#Anuncio

$MELI is quietly executing one of the most dominant growth strategies in global tech by combining a sprawling ecom marketplace with a highly profitable fintech ecosystem. In a recent interview, Mercado Pago's Head of Growth, Tomás Piuzzi, revealed the core drivers propelling the company forward.

First is the massive untapped market in Brazil and Mexico. While Mercado Pago has heavily penetrated Argentina with 22 to 24 million users, it has penetrated Brazil a lot less with a much larger population of 210 million. This leaves an enormous runway for continued user acquisition in the region's largest economies.

To capture this market, Mercado Pago is shifting its brand from a payment gateway to a full digital bank. The growth team is intentionally moving away from vanity metrics like app downloads and focusing entirely on high-value product adoption, specifically credit cards and interest-bearing accounts. This strategy drastically increases the average revenue per user. The credit portfolio is the engine of this growth. Piuzzi noted that merchants utilizing Mercado Credito grow their sales by 30% to 40% more than those who do not. Recent financial disclosures from Business Wire confirm this success, reporting that by Q4 2025, MELI’s credit portfolio surged 90% YoY to $12.5 B, while maintaining a historically low credit card 15-90-day NPL rate of 4.4%.

This creates a powerful, self-reinforcing ecosystem modeled after Asian super apps like WeChat. The ecom platform, which drove $19.9 billion in GMV in Q4, acts as a frictionless, low CAC funnel for the fintech side. 

The company’s organizational agility also serves as a massive competitive moat. Despite scaling to over 100,000 employees, MercadoLibre operates with startup velocity. They have successfully maintained over 30% revenue growth for 28 consecutive quarters, most recently posting 45% YoY revenue growth in Q4.

$MELI is targeting Colombia's digital infrastructure. It is actively eliminating cash on delivery payments. COD orders fail ~50% of the time and destroy profit margins. Pre-pandemic COD rates were ~20%. They are now under ~5%. Mercado Pago acts as the real growth engine. It analyzes over 5,000 variables to stop fraud. It approves ~90% of legitimate transactions for merchants. Colombia's digital payment market is only at a small fraction of its full potential. The core strategy is to use marketplace traffic to build a dominant fintech network.

$MELI's platform is not avoiding social commerce. MercadoLibre introduced short-form video Clips in 2023 and also provides broadcast channels. Sellers are now building proprietary audiences directly within the app. Once a seller gains followers, they can send push notifications and exclusive discount coupons directly to those users. This creates a CAC of essentially zero for repeat buyers and builds a massive competitive moat.

Local logistics are beating centralized warehouses. Wall Street closely tracks MercadoLibre's full centralized fulfillment network. But on the ground in dense urban centers, buyers want instant gratification. Independent sellers using Mercado Envios Flex to deliver items on the exact same day via local couriers are capturing share and out-competing the centralized warehouse infrastructure.

Sellers are using combos to protect margins and dominate ads. Cheap imported goods are causing a price war on the platform. To survive, top sellers are bundling items to artificially raise their AOV. Izts difficult to run Mercado Ads on a low-ticket item because the cost-per-click destroys the margin. By bundling products into kits, sellers increase their margins enough to out-spend competitors on advertising and dominate search rankings.

The latest financial data proves the aggressive growth strategy is working. The market worried about margin pressure when MercadoLibre lowered its free shipping threshold to R$19 in Brazil. But Q4 earnings show this was a highly successful move. The lower threshold drove massive volume, leading to a 45% YoY revenue surge. At the same time, the Mercado Pago credit portfolio grew by 90% YoY to $12.5 B, which means the sticky ecosystem is growing in lock-step.

MercadoLibre is rapidly evolving into a high-margin advertising and credit powerhouse. The core e-commerce marketplace is shifting to a top-of-funnel mechanism that feeds these highly profitable engines.

🥇 Mercado Pago (até 140% do CDI)

O saldo da conta já rende 100% do CDI automaticamente, todos os dias úteis. Mas dá pra ir muito além disso:

→ Deposita R$ 1.000/mês: Cofrinho sobe pra 115% do CDI (até R$ 5 mil) → Assina o Meli+ (R$ 9,90/mês): Cofrinho vai pra 120% do CDI (até R$ 10 mil) → Promoção ativa até 03/04/2026 para assinantes Meli+: 140% do CDI no Cofrinho (até R$ 10 mil por CPF) 

✅ Liquidez diária: Saca quando quiser sem perder rendimento
✅ Sem IOF, sem taxa - só IR sobre o ganho
❌ O 140% é promoção com prazo - depois volta ao 120% para Meli+
❌ Acima do limite de R$ 10 mil, o excedente rende apenas 100% do CDI

Exemplo real: R$ 1.000 no Cofrinho com Meli+ por 12 meses rendem cerca de R$ 179 brutos. Na promoção de 140%, esse valor sobe pra R$ 208.

Pra quem é: quem já usa o Mercado Livre no dia a dia ou quer espremer o máximo de rendimento agora.
